title: lecanthus wightii
intro: Lecanthus wightii, also known as the Indian gentian, is a type of flowering plant native to the Western Ghats in India. The plant has been traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Lecanthus wightii include its potential to aid in digestion, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma. Herbal preparations of the plant include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which can be used to treat various health conditions.
